original: Donald Boyles sets record for highest parachute jump from a bridge, by leaping off of 1,053' Royal George Bridge in Colorado
Drury Gallagher sets fastest swim around Manhattan (6h41m35s)
St. Louis Cardinals outfielder Mark Whiten hits 4 HRs & 12 RBIs in 15-2 win at Cincinnati Reds
Johnny Gruelle patents his Raggedy Ann doll (US Patent D47789)
Strongest Hurricane of century in Netherlands (wind force 12)
Outfielder Don Grate throws a baseball a record 434'1" (Tenn)
Space Shuttle Endeavour (STS-69) launches into orbit
British archaeologists announce the discovery of a Neolithic "superhenge" under 3ft of earth at Durrington Walls
Donald Henderson, American epidemiologist, led world campaign that eradicated smallpox, born in Lakewood, Ohio (d. 2016)
Philo Farnsworth demonstrates the first use of his electronic television in San Francisco
American Rod Milburn with a world record 13.24 wins the 110m hurdles gold medal at the Munich Olympics; just 0.10 seconds ahead of Frenchman Guy Drut
1st game of Boston Massacre, NY Yankees beat Red Sox, 15-3
Entertainment and Sports Programming Network (ESPN) launches, first show is SportsCenter
Asteroid 2014 RC makes a close approach to Earth (39,900 km; 24,800 mi)
Discovery of the earliest evidence of surgery from 31,000 year old skeleton with amputated lower leg in a cave in East Kalimantan, Borneo published in "Nature"
Jimmy Page,Robert Plant,John Paul JonesandJohn Bonhammake live debut as Led Zeppelin (but billed as The New Yardbirds), before about 1,200 students at Egegard School 'Teen Club' in Gladsaxe, Denmark
Intense flooding caused by the heaviest rainfall in 140 years in Hong Kong, Shenzhen and southern Chinese cities turns streets into rivers
Bell X-2 sets Unofficial manned aircraft altitude record 126,000'+
Japan wins the men's gymnastics teams combined exercises gold for the first of 5 consecutive Olympics at the Rome Games; beats Soviet Union by just 2.5 points
Ljudmila Shevcova runs female olympic record 800m (2:04.3)
East German sprinter Renate Stecher runs a world record equalling 22.40 in the 200m final to repeat her 100m win over Australia's Raelene Boyle at the Munich Olympics
Hurricane Ivan, a Category 5 hurricane, hits Grenada, killing 39 people and damaging 90% of its buildings
Indian Space Agency loses contact with its Chandrayaan-2 moon lander just 2 km from landing on the Moon's surface
James Corbett KOs 4 to 1 favourite and defending championJohn L. Sullivanin 21 rounds at the Olympic Club, New Orleans for world heavyweight boxing title; Sullivan's only defeat and his last fight
Boulder Dam (nowHooverDam), on the Colorado River, between Nevada and Arizona begins operation
Rashid Khantakes the first hat-trick in the history of the Caribbean Premier League
Solar eclipse on this date might indicate the birth of the legendary Heracles in Thebes, Greece
Giants beat Brooklyn 4-1, to launch NY's record 26-game winning streak
US sweeps the medals in the men's discus at the Rome Olympics;Al Oerterwins his 2nd of 4 career discus gold ahead of fellow Americans Rink Babka and Dick Cochran
Joe Kuhel hits inside-the-park HR, only HR hit by a Senator all season at Washington's Griffith Stadium
NY Yankees Johnny Mize's pinch-hit grand slam gives Yanks a 5-1 win at Washington, giving him a HR in all 15 major league parks
Future Constantine II, last King of Greece, wins Dragon class sailing gold medal at the Rome Olympics
American Bandstand moves to California, & airs once a week on Saturday
Cincinnati Reds, win earliest NL division title
Earnest Gray becomes the second NY Giant to score four touchdowns (vs St. Louis)
Mary Decker Tabb Slaney runs US 3K female record (8:25.83)
Cleveland Browns become 1st team in NFL history to have a play reviewed by instant replay, Chicago 41, Browns 31
1st South African international competition in 25 years, gymnastics
Scottish Matra-Ford driverJackie Stewartwins the Italian Grand Prix at Monza to clinch his first Formula 1 World Drivers Championship; his 6th F1 win of the season
Dan Marinothrows his 100th career touchdown pass, the fastest QB in NFL history to do so
Edith Eleanor McLean is the first baby to be placed in an incubator, at State Emigrant Hospital on Ward's Island, New York
Olive Oatman, American (as a teen captured and released by Native Americans), born in La Harpe, Illinois (d. 1903)
Don Messick, American cartoon voice actor (The Flintstones - "Bamm-Bamm"; The Jetsons - "Astro"; Scooby-Doo), born in Buffalo, New York (d. 1997)
In Australia, the whole nation observes a 'day of humiliation' and prays for rain as a terrible drought kills livestock and threatens crops; rain begins to fall on September 10
Earl Grace, ends NL catcher record streak of 110 cons errorless games
Danish yachtsman Paul Elvstrøm wins the Finn class at the Rome Olympics; his 4th straight Olympic gold medal in the category
American athletes Vincent Matthews & Wayne Collett finish 1-2 in 400m at the Munich Olympics; acted casually on the medal stand, did not face the flag during anthem; banned from the Olympics for life
Jingyi Le, Ying Shan, Ying Le and Bin Lu swimming 4x100 freestyle (3:37.91)
15th Summer Paralympics opens in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil
New York YankeeWhitey Fordbecomes 5th MLB pitcher to hurl consecutive 1 hitters
James Van Allen, American physicist (discovered Van Allen radiation belts), born in Mount Pleasant, Iowa (d. 2006)
Eric Hill, British children's books author and illustrator (Where's Spot), born in Holloway, London (d. 2014)
"Uncle Sam" is first used to refer to the US, by Troy Post of New York
A. H. Whiting wins the first automobile race held on a closed-circuit track in Cranston, Rhode Island
Ocean liner RMS Lusitania begins her maiden voyage sailing from Liverpool to New York City
St Louis Terriers' Dave Davenport no-hits Chicago Whales (Federal League), 3-0
Integration begins in Washington, D.C. & Baltimore, Maryland public schools
Nadezhda Chizhova of the Soviet Union throws a world record 21.03m to win the women's shot put at the Munich Olympics
Die Laughing wins Messenger Stakes
Roy Campanellasets catcher record of 125 (en route to 142) RBIs
MetDwight Gooden's 11 strikeouts gives him NL rookie record 236
Gloria Gaynor [Fowles], American disco singer ("I Will Survive"; "I Am What I Am"), born in Newark, New Jersey
The last surviving member of the marsupial thylacine species, Benjamin, dies alone in his/her cage at the Hobart Zoo (Hobart, Tasmania, Australia)
Two-building Pro Football Hall of Fame in Canton, Ohio is officially opened
White Sox use record 41 players in doubleheader & lose both games
Future world professional cycling road race champion Hennie Kuiper of the Netherlands wins the road race gold medal at the Munich Olympics in 4:14:37
Oakland A's pitch record 78th complete game of season
First test flight of the USAF F-22 Raptor takes place
64 people are killed and 715 injured after a series of earthquakes in south-west China
Wildfires have burnt a record 2 million acres in California 2020 fire season, more than the state of Delaware according to Cal Fire
Pedro I, son of King John VI, declares Brazil's independence from Portugal (National Day)
Luigi Galvani, Italian anatomist and physicist ("animal electricity"), born in Bologna, Italy (d. 1798)
Geo Ligowsky patents device to throw clay pigeons for trapshooters
Federation of American Motorcyclists organized in NY

American weightlifter Charles Vinci wins 2nd consecutive bantamweight gold medal at the Rome Olympics with world-record equalling 3-lift (snatch, clean & jerk, overhead press) total of 345.0 kilograms
Brigham Young University quarterback Ty Detmer passes NCAA record 11,606 yards
Heinrich David Stölzel, German horn player who developed the first valve for brass instruments, born in Schneeberg, Electrorate of Saxony, Holy Roman Empire (d. 1844)
Jacob Lawrence, African-American artist (Sanitarium), born in Atlantic City, New Jersey (d. 2000)
{Walter) "Sonny" Rollins, American Grammy Ward-winning jazz saxophonist ("Blue Room"), environmentalist, and last survivor of iconic "A Great Fay in Harlem" photograph, born in New York City[1]
City of Boston, Massachusetts, is founded
George Davis (Giants) HR off Sport McAllister (Spiders) in DH
Cleveland Browns' Brian Sipe sets club record with 57 pass attempts
Julie Kavner, American actress and voice artist (Brenda-Rhoda, Marge-Simpsons), born in Los Angeles, California
Boston Red Sox Howard Ehmke no-hits Philadelphia A's, 4-0
Radio NY Worldwide-WRUL begins radio transmission
August Kekule von Stradonitz, German chemist who discovered structure of benzene ring, born in Darmstadt, rand Duchy of Hesse by the Rhine, German Confederation (d. 1896)
Stanley David Griggs, American astronaut (STS 51-D), STS 33), born in Portland, Oregon (d. 1989)
KTNE TV channel 13 in Alliance, NB (PBS) begins broadcasting
Thomas Curtis, American athlete (Olympic 1896 Gold), born in San Francisco, California (d. 1944)
John Shea, American 500m/1500m speed skater (Olympic gold 1932), born in Lake Placid, New York (d. 2002)
